entry.title: Cannot connect Raspberry Pi after a period of time?
entry.content:
These days, humidifier in the study is working day and night since I wanna reduce formaldehyde content(that’s another story). Around one week later, ghosting appeared on my display screen and finally the root cause turned out to be the humidifier’s water vapor. Moving the monitor to the balcony and under the sunshine, then it works well again.

What drives me crazy is that my Raspberry Pi refused to work almost at the same time! I used to connect my Raspberry Pi via SSH while this time it showed “connection refused”. I was afraid that it was due to the vapor as well and disappointed when it still had no response after one day’s in the sun.

This Saturday, getting time to dig further, I connected the mouse, keyboard and monitor to my lovely Pi, and it’s working! How came I could not SSH to it? It’s the IP address changing!

By default, the Raspberry Pi will get a dynamically allocated IP address, meaning it changes as you restart it or potentially when new devices are added to the network. We could set a static IP address to solve this issue.

[reference] https://www.ionos.com/digitalguide/server/configuration/provide-raspberry-pi-with-a-static-ip-address/

Copyright © 2022- Emily Peng All Rights Reserved

entry.title: VIM - common used cmd!
entry.content:
case 1 - to delete and add same content in multi lines

before change

add

  1. move the cursor at the first line first character
  2. ctrl + v and enter visual block mode
  3. use motion key or run “3gg” to select all
  4. input I (upper case) to enter Edit mode
  5. input the content you want to insert
  6. ESC and wait for a moment

after change

delete

  1. move the cursor at the first line first character
  2. ctrl + v and enter visual block mode
  3. use motion key or run “3j3l” to select what you want to delete
  4. enter “d” to delete
    tip: after delete, we could still use ‘p’ to paste the deleted sentence which means ‘delete’ is like ‘cut’.

Copyright © 2022- Emily Peng All Rights Reserved

categories.category: Real-Time Embedded Systems Concepts and Practices

entry.title: How to use hexo to create page, tag and category
entry.content:
Once setting up the hexo environment, it’s time to create your new pages! Shall we just use mkdir cmd to create subfolders under source folder? The answer is NO. Please realize this is HEXO! We could use “hexo cmd” directly!

1. create new file

  • cmd
    hexo new <title>

the file would be generated under hexo/source/_posts folder

  • settings

2. create new tag

  • cmd
    hexo new page tags

  • settings

3. create new category

  • cmd
    hexo new page categories

  • settings

for more information, please go to hexo website

Copyright © 2022- Emily Peng All Rights Reserved
